text: When you encounter a bug in your code, the first step is identifying the problem. Try to reproduce the error consistently. A systematic approach helps: check your recent changes, isolate the faulty section, and test each part separately. Debugging tools like breakpoints in an IDE let you pause execution and inspect variables. Logging is also valuable; by printing messages at different points, you can track the flow of data and spot where things go wrong. With practice, these strategies turn debugging from a frustrating search into a logical, solvable puzzle.
